mission.title: kubeflow: Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namespaces I own
mission.description: Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namespaces I own. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
1. I set up a Kubeflow 0.7 cluster and get a `gongyuan` namespace.
2. I created a profile `demo` which also marks me as the owner.
3.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the
steps.description:
Estimated efforts is 1~1.5 weeks, we need the relevant updates to the 2 other components, namespace selector (trivial if any), registration flow (also perhaps trivial), and tests

### Some notes for how to develop
* First time only:
```
npm i
make build-local
npm run build
```
* Run the commands in
```yaml
X-Goog-Authenticated-User-Email: accounts.google.com:kubeflow-user@k
```

steps.title: Confirm Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Estimated efforts is 1~1.5 weeks, we need the relevant updates to the 2 other components, namespace selector (trivial if any), registration flow (also perhaps trivial), and tests

### Some notes for how to develop
* First time only:
```
npm i
make build-local
npm run build
```
* Run the commands in two separate terminals: `npm run dev` and `kubectl port-forward svc/profiles-kfam 8081:8081`

###

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
